tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and high urine protein and ketones, which may indicate renal or metabolic stress. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity, but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c suggests absence of chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function and possible further nephrological evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
